title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards by outlining its plans to address the concerns and recommendations made by the committee. The response aims to improve public trust in AI and ensure its safe and responsible use in various sectors.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This policy document outlines the Department for Education's stance on using generative artificial intelligence in educational settings. It likely discusses guidelines and regulations for implementing and ensuring responsible use of AI in educational institutions.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: Ofqual, a qualifications regulator, is developing a policy to oversee the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. This policy aims to ensure the responsible and effective use of AI in the qualifications sector.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyrighted materials in the development of AI systems, exploring the potential consequences for copyright holders and AI creators. It aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the complex relationships between copyright and artificial intelligence.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence. They provided their thoughts and proposals on how to address the concerns and challenges raised in the report 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing how artificial intelligence relates to public standards. The committee will also be answering frequently asked questions about this topic.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Centre for Data Ethics and Transformation (no details given about CSPL) has submitted information to a government committee looking into how artificial intelligence is controlled. They provided this written evidence as part of an inquiry into the governance of AI.

title: Impact of AI on the regulation of medical products
description: Implementing the Artificial Intelligence (AI) White Paper principles.
summary: This AI policy document discusses how emerging AI technologies will affect the regulation of medical products. It aims to implement the principles outlined in an Artificial Intelligence White Paper to address these changes.

title: Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy
description: Our vision is that, in terms of AI, we will be the world’s most effective, efficient, trusted and influential Defence organisation for our size.
summary: The Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy aims to make the defence organisation the world's most effective and efficient in using AI, while also being trusted and influential. This vision will be achieved by utilising artificial intelligence to improve performance and competitiveness.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport AI Action Plan outlines how the Department for Transport will work with the transport sector to utilize the benefits of artificial intelligence. It aims to balance the potential advantages and risks associated with AI in the transport industry.
